content.title: The White Horse of Uffington
content.notes: ['*Iron Age: a period (in Britain 800 BCE – 43 CE) that is characterised by the use of iron tools', '*Bronze Age: a period (in Britain c. 2,500 BCE – 800 BCE) that is characterised by the development of bronze tools', '**Neolithic: a period (in Britain c. 4,000 BCE – c. 2,500 BCE) that is significant for the spread of agricultural practices, and the use of stone tools', '***Celtic: an ancient people who migrated from Europe to Britain before the Romans']

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: The cutting of huge figures or 'geoglyphs' into the earth of English hillsides has taken place for more than 3,000 years. There are 56 hill figures scattered around England, with the vast majority on the chalk downlands of the country's southern counties. The figures include giants, horses, crosses and regimental badges. Although the majority of these geoglyphs date within the last 300 years or so, there are one or two that are much older.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: The most famous of these figures is perhaps also the most mysterious – the Uffington White Horse in Oxfordshire. The White Horse has recently been re-dated and shown to be even older than its previously assigned ancient pre-Roman Iron Age* date. More controversial is the date of the enigmatic Long Man of Wilmington in Sussex. While many historians are convinced the figure is prehistoric, others believe that it was the work of an artistic monk from a nearby priory and was created between the 11th and 15th centuries.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: The method of cutting these huge figures was simply to remove the overlying grass to reveal the gleaming white chalk below. However, the grass would soon grow over the geoglyph again unless it was regularly cleaned or scoured by a fairly large team of people. One reason that the vast majority of hill figures have disappeared is that when the traditions associated with the figures faded, people no longer bothered or remembered to clear away the grass to expose the chalk outline. Furthermore, over hundreds of years the outlines would sometimes change due to people not always cutting in exactly the same place, thus creating a different shape to the original geoglyph. The fact that any ancient hill figures survive at all in England today is testament to the strength and continuity of local customs and beliefs which, in one case at least, must stretch back over millennia.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: The Uffington White Horse is a unique, stylised representation of a horse consisting of a long, sleek back, thin disjointed legs, a streaming tail, and a bird-like beaked head. The elegant creature almost melts into the landscape. The horse is situated 2.5 km from Uffington village on a steep slope close to the Late Bronze Age* (c. 7th century BCE) hillfort of Uffington Castle and below the Ridgeway, a long-distance Neolithic** track.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: The Uffington Horse is also surrounded by Bronze Age burial mounds. It is not far from the Bronze Age cemetery of Lambourn Seven Barrows, which consists of more than 30 well-preserved burial mounds. The carving has been placed in such a way as to make it extremely difficult to see from close quarters, and like many geoglyphs is best appreciated from the air. Nevertheless, there are certain areas of the Vale of the White Horse, the valley containing and named after the enigmatic creature, from which an adequate impression may be gained. Indeed on a clear day the carving can be seen from up to 30 km away.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: The earliest evidence of a horse at Uffington is from the 1070s CE when 'White Horse Hill' is mentioned in documents from the nearby Abbey of Abingdon, and the first reference to the horse itself is soon after, in 1190 CE. However, the carving is believed to date back much further than that. Due to the similarity of the Uffington White Horse to the stylised depictions of horses on 1st century BCE coins, it had been thought that the creature must also date to that period.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: However, in 1995 Optically Stimulated Luminescence (OSL) testing was carried out by the Oxford Archaeological Unit on soil from two of the lower layers of the horse's body, and from another cut near the base. The result was a date for the horse's construction somewhere between 1400 and 600 BCE – in other words, it had a Late Bronze Age or Early Iron Age origin.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: The latter end of this date range would tie the carving of the horse in with occupation of the nearby Uffington hillfort, indicating that it may represent a tribal emblem marking the land of the inhabitants of the hillfort. Alternatively, the carving may have been carried out during a Bronze or Iron Age ritual. Some researchers see the horse as representing the Celtic*** horse goddess Epona, who was worshipped as a protector of horses, and for her associations with fertility. However, the cult of Epona was not imported from Gaul (France) until around the first century CE. This date is at least six centuries after the Uffington Horse was probably carved. Nevertheless, the horse had great ritual and economic significance during the Bronze and Iron Ages, as attested by its depictions on jewellery and other metal objects. It is possible that the carving represents a goddess in native mythology, such as Rhiannon, described in later Welsh mythology as a beautiful woman dressed in gold and riding a white horse.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: The fact that geoglyphs can disappear easily, along with their associated rituals and meaning, indicates that they were never intended to be anything more than temporary gestures. But this does not lessen their importance. These giant carvings are a fascinating glimpse into the minds of their creators and how they viewed the landscape in which they lived.

groups.desc:
Questions 1-8

Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 1?

In questions1–8, choose
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information 
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information 
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this

questions.title: Most geoglyphs in England are located in a particular area of the country.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Most geoglyphs”对应原文中的“the vast majority”，“located in a particular area”对应原文中的“on the chalk downlands of the country's southern counties”。原文明确指出绝大多数地画位于英格兰南部郡的白垩丘陵地带，这与题干信息一致，因此答案是TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: There are 56 hill figures scattered around England, with the vast majority on the chalk downlands of the country's southern counties.
central_sentences.translation: 英格兰各地散布着56个山坡图形，其中绝大多数位于该国南部各郡的白垩质丘陵地带。

target_sen.target_sen: There are 56 hill figures scattered around England, with the vast majority on the chalk downlands of the country's southern counties.

syn_ary.synonym: the chalk downlands of the country's southern counties

target_sen.target_sen: There are 56 hill figures scattered around England, with the vast majority on the chalk downlands of the country's southern counties.

questions.title: There are more geoglyphs in the shape of a horse than any other creature.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“马形状的地画比其他任何生物的地画都多”。在原文中，提到了不同形状的地画，包括巨人、马、十字架和军团徽章，但并没有具体说明哪种形状的地画数量最多。因此，文章没有提供足够的信息来支持题干中的陈述，正确答案是 NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: The figures include giants, horses, crosses and regimental badges.
central_sentences.translation: 这些图形包括巨人、马、十字架和军团徽章。

target_sen.target_sen: The cutting of huge figures or 'geoglyphs' into the earth of English hillsides has taken place for more than 3,000 years.
Although the majority of these geoglyphs date within the last 300 years or so, there are one or two that are much older.
The cutting of huge figures or 'geoglyphs' into the earth of English hillsides has taken place for more than 3,000 years.
There are 56 hill figures scattered around England, with the vast majority on the chalk downlands of the country's southern counties.
The figures include giants, horses, crosses and regimental badges.
The figures include giants, horses, crosses and regimental badges.

questions.title: A recent dating of the Uffington White Horse indicates that people were mistaken about its age.
questions.analysis: 答案是 TRUE，因为题干中的 "recent dating" 对应文章中的 "recently been re-dated"，而 "mistaken about its age" 对应文章中的 "even older than its previously assigned date"。这表明最近的测定显示乌芬顿白马的年代比之前认为的更古老，证明之前的看法是错误的。

central_sentences.sentence: The White Horse has recently been re-dated and shown to be even older than its previously assigned ancient pre-Roman Iron Age* date.
central_sentences.translation: 白马最近被重新测定年代，结果显示它比之前认为的古代前罗马铁器时代的年代更为古老。

target_sen.target_sen: The White Horse has recently been re-dated and shown to be even older than its previously assigned ancient pre-Roman Iron Age* date.

syn_ary.synonym: shown to be even older than its previously assigned ancient pre-Roman Iron Age* date

target_sen.target_sen: The White Horse has recently been re-dated and shown to be even older than its previously assigned ancient pre-Roman Iron Age* date.

questions.title: Historians have come to an agreement about the origins of the Long Man of Wilmington.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Historians have come to an agreement about the origins of the Long Man of Wilmington" 表示历史学家已经就威尔明顿长人的起源达成了一致意见。然而，原文中明确提到 "More controversial is the date of the enigmatic Long Man of Wilmington in Sussex" 和 "While many historians are convinced the figure is prehistoric, others believe that it was the work of an artistic monk from a nearby priory and was created between the 11th and 15th centuries." 这表明关于威尔明顿长人的起源存在争议，不同历史学家有不同的看法。因此，题干中的信息是错误的，正确答案为 FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: More controversial is the date of the enigmatic Long Man of Wilmington in Sussex. While many historians are convinced the figure is prehistoric, others believe that it was the work of an artistic monk from a nearby priory and was created between the 11th and 15th centuries.
central_sentences.translation: 苏塞克斯郡的威尔明顿长人的年代更具争议性。虽然许多历史学家确信这个图形是史前的，但其他人则认为它是附近修道院的一位艺术僧侣的作品，创作于11至15世纪之间。

target_sen.target_sen: While many historians are convinced the figure is prehistoric, others believe that it was the work of an artistic monk from a nearby priory and was created between the 11th and 15th centuries.
More controversial is the date of the enigmatic Long Man of Wilmington in Sussex.
The White Horse has recently been re-dated and shown to be even older than its previously assigned ancient pre-Roman Iron Age* date.
More controversial is the date of the enigmatic Long Man of Wilmington in Sussex.
More controversial is the date of the enigmatic Long Man of Wilmington in Sussex.

questions.title: Geoglyphs were created by people placing white chalk on the hillside.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“Geoglyphs were created by people placing white chalk on the hillside.”，意思是地画是通过人们在山坡上放置白色粉笔来创造的。而原文中明确指出，地画是通过移除覆盖的草来露出下面的白色粉笔（白垩）而形成的。这与题干中的信息相反，因此答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: The method of cutting these huge figures was simply to remove the overlying grass to reveal the gleaming white chalk below.
central_sentences.translation: 制作这些巨大图形的方法很简单，就是移除覆盖的草，露出下面闪亮的白色粉笔。

target_sen.target_sen: The method of cutting these huge figures was simply to remove the overlying grass to reveal the gleaming white chalk below.

One reason that the vast majority of hill figures have disappeared is that when the traditions associated with the figures faded, people no longer bothered or remembered to clear away the grass to expose the chalk outline.

The fact that any ancient hill figures survive at all in England today is testament to the strength and continuity of local customs and beliefs which, in one case at least, must stretch back over millennia.
The method of cutting these huge figures was simply to remove the overlying grass to reveal the gleaming white chalk below.
Furthermore, over hundreds of years the outlines would sometimes change due to people not always cutting in exactly the same place, thus creating a different shape to the original geoglyph.

syn_ary.synonym: remove the overlying grass to reveal the gleaming white chalk below

target_sen.target_sen: The method of cutting these huge figures was simply to remove the overlying grass to reveal the gleaming white chalk below.

questions.title: Many geoglyphs in England are no longer visible.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Many geoglyphs in England are no longer visible”意思是“很多英格兰的地面雕刻已经不再可见”。在原文中，提到“大多数的山地雕刻已经消失了”（the vast majority of hill figures have disappeared），这与题干中的“no longer visible”是相同的意思。因此，答案为TRUE。通过找到题干中的关键词“geoglyphs”和“no longer visible”，并在原文中找到对应的同义替换词“hill figures”和“have disappeared”，可以确定答案。

central_sentences.sentence: One reason that the vast majority of hill figures have disappeared is that when the traditions associated with the figures faded, people no longer bothered or remembered to clear away the grass to expose the chalk outline.
central_sentences.translation: 山地雕刻消失的一个原因是，当与这些图形相关的传统逐渐消失时，人们不再费心或记得清除草地以露出粉笔轮廓。

target_sen.target_sen: One reason that the vast majority of hill figures have disappeared is that when the traditions associated with the figures faded, people no longer bothered or remembered to clear away the grass to expose the chalk outline.

The fact that any ancient hill figures survive at all in England today is testament to the strength and continuity of local customs and beliefs which, in one case at least, must stretch back over millennia.

One reason that the vast majority of hill figures have disappeared is that when the traditions associated with the figures faded, people no longer bothered or remembered to clear away the grass to expose the chalk outline.

questions.title: The shape of some geoglyphs has been altered over time.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“shape”对应文章中的“outlines”，“altered”对应“change”，“over time”对应“over hundreds of years”。这些同义表达确认了题干信息在文章中可以找到。因此，题干信息在文章中得到了验证，答案为TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: Furthermore, over hundreds of years the outlines would sometimes change due to people not always cutting in exactly the same place, thus creating a different shape to the original geoglyph.
central_sentences.translation: 此外，在数百年的时间里，由于人们并不总是在完全相同的位置进行切割，轮廓有时会发生变化，从而创造出与原始地表图形不同的形状。

target_sen.target_sen: Furthermore, over hundreds of years the outlines would sometimes change due to people not always cutting in exactly the same place, thus creating a different shape to the original geoglyph.
Furthermore, over hundreds of years the outlines would sometimes change due to people not always cutting in exactly the same place, thus creating a different shape to the original geoglyph.
Furthermore, over hundreds of years the outlines would sometimes change due to people not always cutting in exactly the same place, thus creating a different shape to the original geoglyph.

questions.title: The fame of the Uffington White Horse is due to its size.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词是“fame”和“size”。在原文中，“fame”对应的同义词是“famous”，但没有找到与“size”相关的同义替换词。原文提到乌芬顿白马的名声和神秘性，但没有提到它的名声是由于其大小。因此，题干中的信息在文章中没有直接或间接的对应表达，正确答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: The most famous of these figures is perhaps also the most mysterious – the Uffington White Horse in Oxfordshire.
central_sentences.translation: 这些图形中最著名的可能也是最神秘的——牛津郡的乌芬顿白马。

target_sen.target_sen: The most famous of these figures is perhaps also the most mysterious – the Uffington White Horse in Oxfordshire.
The most famous of these figures is perhaps also the most mysterious – the Uffington White Horse in Oxfordshire.

groups.title: The Uffington White Horse
groups.desc:
Questions 9-13

Complete the notes below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer. Write your answers in boxes 9–13.
groups.collect: The location of the Uffington White Horse: • a distance of 2.5 km from Uffington village • near an ancient road known as the$369$ • close to an ancient cemetery that has a number of burial mounds Dating the Uffington White Horse: • first reference to White Horse Hill appears in $370$from the 1070s • horses shown on coins from the period 100 BCE – 1 BCE are similar in appearance • according to analysis of the surrounding $371$, the Horse is Late Bronze Age / Early Iron Age Possible reasons for creation of the Uffington White Horse: • an emblem to indicate land ownership • formed part of an ancient ritual • was a representation of goddess Epona – associated with protection of horses and $372$ • was a representation of a Welsh goddess called $373$

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "ancient road" 对应原文中的 "Neolithic track"，"known as" 对应原文中的 "called" 或 "named"。通过这些线索词，我们可以定位到答案句。答案句中明确提到 "Ridgeway" 是一个古老的道路，因此答案是 "Ridgeway"。

central_sentences.sentence: The horse is situated 2.5 km from Uffington village on a steep slope close to the Late Bronze Age* (c. 7th century BCE) hillfort of Uffington Castle and below the Ridgeway, a long-distance Neolithic** track.
central_sentences.translation: 这匹马位于距离阿芬顿村2.5公里的陡坡上，靠近晚青铜时代（约公元前7世纪）的阿芬顿城堡山堡，并位于里奇韦（一条长距离的新石器时代道路）的下方。

target_sen.target_sen: The horse is situated 2.5 km from Uffington village on a steep slope close to the Late Bronze Age* (c. 7th century BCE) hillfort of Uffington Castle and below the Ridgeway, a long-distance Neolithic** track.

questions.analysis: 答案是“documents”，因为题干中的“first reference”对应原文中的“earliest evidence”，“White Horse Hill”在原文中直接提到，而“1070s”对应“1070s CE”。原文句子明确指出“White Horse Hill”在1070年代的首次提及是在“documents”中。因此，答案是“documents”。

central_sentences.sentence: The earliest evidence of a horse at Uffington is from the 1070s CE when 'White Horse Hill' is mentioned in documents from the nearby Abbey of Abingdon, and the first reference to the horse itself is soon after, in 1190 CE.
central_sentences.translation: 在阿宾顿附近修道院的文件中提到"白马山"，这是乌芬顿马形地画最早的证据，可以追溯到公元1070年代。而对马形地画本身的第一次提及则稍晚，在公元1190年。

target_sen.target_sen: The earliest evidence of a horse at Uffington is from the 1070s CE when 'White Horse Hill' is mentioned in documents from the nearby Abbey of Abingdon, and the first reference to the horse itself is soon after, in 1190 CE.

The earliest evidence of a horse at Uffington is from the 1070s CE when 'White Horse Hill' is mentioned in documents from the nearby Abbey of Abingdon, and the first reference to the horse itself is soon after, in 1190 CE.

questions.analysis: 答案是“soil”，因为题干中的“analysis”对应文章中的“testing”，“surrounding”对应文章中的“from two of the lower layers of the horse's body, and from another cut near the base”，而“Late Bronze Age / Early Iron Age”对应文章中的“Late Bronze Age or Early Iron Age origin”。通过这些对应关系，我们可以确定答案句中的“soil”是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: However, in 1995 Optically Stimulated Luminescence (OSL) testing was carried out by the Oxford Archaeological Unit on soil from two of the lower layers of the horse's body, and from another cut near the base. The result was a date for the horse's construction somewhere between 1400 and 600 BCE – in other words, it had a Late Bronze Age or Early Iron Age origin.
central_sentences.translation: 然而，在1995年，牛津考古单位对马身体下层的两处土壤样本和底部附近的一处切割样本进行了光释光(OSL)测试。测试结果显示，这匹马的建造时间在公元前1400年到600年之间——换句话说，它起源于晚期青铜时代或早期铁器时代。

target_sen.target_sen: However, in 1995 Optically Stimulated Luminescence (OSL) testing was carried out by the Oxford Archaeological Unit on soil from two of the lower layers of the horse's body, and from another cut near the base.

syn_ary.synonym: Late Bronze Age or Early Iron Age origin

target_sen.target_sen: The result was a date for the horse's construction somewhere between 1400 and 600 BCE – in other words, it had a Late Bronze Age or Early Iron Age origin.

locating_words.locating_word: Late Bronze Age / Early Iron Age

questions.analysis: 题干中的“goddess Epona”和“protection of horses”在原文中直接对应“goddess Epona”和“protector of horses”。题干中的“and”也在原文中直接对应。通过这些线索词，我们可以定位到原文中的答案句。答案句中明确提到Epona与“fertility”相关联，因此答案是“fertility”。

central_sentences.sentence: Some researchers see the horse as representing the Celtic*** horse goddess Epona, who was worshipped as a protector of horses, and for her associations with fertility.
central_sentences.translation: 一些研究人员认为这匹马代表了凯尔特马神埃波娜，她被崇拜为马匹的保护者，并与生育能力有关。

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Welsh goddess" 对应文章中的 "Welsh mythology"，而 "called" 对应文章中的 "such as"。通过这些对应关系，可以确定答案所在句子，并确认答案是 "Rhiannon"。文章明确指出 Rhiannon 是威尔士神话中的女神，因此答案是正确的。

central_sentences.sentence: It is possible that the carving represents a goddess in native mythology, such as Rhiannon, described in later Welsh mythology as a beautiful woman dressed in gold and riding a white horse.
central_sentences.translation: 这个雕刻可能代表了本土神话中的一位女神，比如莱安农，她在后来的威尔士神话中被描述为一位身着金衣、骑着白马的美丽女子。

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Microbes, most of them bacteria, have populated this planet since long before animal life developed and they will outlive us. Invisible to the naked eye, they are ubiquitous. They inhabit the soil, air, rocks and water and are present within every form of life, from seaweed and coral to dogs and humans. And, as Yong explains in his utterly absorbing and hugely important book, we mess with them at our peril.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Every species has its own colony of microbes, called a 'microbiome', and these microbes vary not only between species but also between individuals and within different parts of each individual. What is amazing is that while the number of human cells in the average person is about 30 trillion, the number of microbial ones is higher – about 39 trillion. At best, Yong informs us, we are only 50 per cent human. Indeed, some scientists even suggest we should think of each species and its microbes as a single unit, dubbed a 'holobiont'.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: In each human there are microbes that live only in the stomach, the mouth or the armpit and by and large they do so peacefully. So 'bad' microbes are just microbes out of context. Microbes that sit contentedly in the human gut (where there are more microbes than there are stars in the galaxy) can become deadly if they find their way into the bloodstream. These communities are constantly changing too. The right hand shares just one sixth of its microbes with the left hand. And, of course, we are surrounded by microbes. Every time we eat, we swallow a million microbes in each gram of food; we are continually swapping microbes with other humans, pets and the world at large.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: It's a fascinating topic and Yong, a young British science journalist, is an extraordinarily adept guide. Writing with lightness and panache, he has a knack of explaining complex science in terms that are both easy to understand and totally enthralling. Yong is on a mission. Leading us gently by the hand, he takes us into the world of microbes – a bizarre, alien planet – in a bid to persuade us to love them as much as he does. By the end, we do.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: For most of human history we had no idea that microbes existed. The first man to see these extraordinarily potent creatures was a Dutch lens-maker called Antony van Leeuwenhoek in the 1670s. Using microscopes of his own design that could magnify up to 270 times, he examined a drop of water from a nearby lake and found it teeming with tiny creatures he called 'animalcules'. It wasn't until nearly two hundred years later that the research of French biologist Louis Pasteur indicated that some microbes caused disease. It was Pasteur's 'germ theory' that gave bacteria the poor image that endures today.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Yong's book is in many ways a plea for microbial tolerance, pointing out that while fewer than one hundred species of bacteria bring disease, many thousands more play a vital role in maintaining our health. The book also acknowledges that our attitude towards bacteria is not a simple one. We tend to see the dangers posed by bacteria, yet at the same time we are sold yoghurts and drinks that supposedly nurture 'friendly' bacteria. In reality, says Yong, bacteria should not be viewed as either friends or foes, villains or heroes. Instead we should realise we have a symbiotic relationship, that can be mutually beneficial or mutually destructive.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: What then do these millions of organisms do? The answer is pretty much everything. New research is now unravelling the ways in which bacteria aid digestion, regulate our immune systems, eliminate toxins, produce vitamins, affect our behaviour and even combat obesity. 'They actually help us become who we are,' says Yong. But we are facing a growing problem. Our obsession with hygiene, our overuse of antibiotics and our unhealthy, low-fibre diets are disrupting the bacterial balance and may be responsible for soaring rates of allergies and immune problems, such as inflammatory bowel disease (IBD).

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: The most recent research actually turns accepted norms upside down. For example, there are studies indicating that the excessive use of household detergents and antibacterial products actually destroys the microbes that normally keep the more dangerous germs at bay. Other studies show that keeping a dog as a pet gives children early exposure to a diverse range of bacteria, which may help protect them against allergies later.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: The readers of Yong's book must be prepared for a decidedly unglamorous world. Among the less appealing case studies is one about a fungus that is wiping out entire populations of frogs and that can be halted by a rare microbial bacterium. Another is about squid that carry luminescent bacteria that protect them against predators. However, if you can overcome your distaste for some of the investigations, the reasons for Yong's enthusiasm become clear. The microbial world is a place of wonder. Already, in an attempt to stop mosquitoes spreading dengue fever – a disease that infects 400 million people a year – mosquitoes are being loaded with a bacterium to block the disease. In the future, our ability to manipulate microbes means we could construct buildings with useful microbes built into their walls to fight off infections. Just imagine a neonatal hospital ward coated in a specially mixed cocktail of microbes so that babies get the best start in life.

groups.desc:
Questions 14–16

Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

questions.title: What point does the writer make about microbes in the first paragraph?

options.text: They adapt quickly to their environment.
The risk they pose has been exaggerated.
They are more plentiful in animal life than plant life.
They will continue to exist for longer than the human race.

questions.analysis: 文章中明确提到 "they will outlive us"，意思是微生物会比人类活得更久，这与选项D "They will continue to exist for longer than the human race" 的意思完全一致。因此，选项D是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Microbes, most of them bacteria, have populated this planet since long before animal life developed and they will outlive us.
central_sentences.translation: 微生物，其中大多数是细菌，在动物生命出现之前很久就已经存在于这个星球上，而且它们将会比我们活得更久。

target_sen.target_sen: Microbes, most of them bacteria, have populated this planet since long before animal life developed and they will outlive us.

questions.title: In the second paragraph, the writer is impressed by the fact that

options.text: each species tends to have vastly different microbes.
some parts of the body contain relatively few microbes.
the average individual has more microbial cells than human ones.
scientists have limited understanding of how microbial cells behave.

questions.analysis: 在第二段中，作者感到惊讶的是普通人身体中的微生物细胞数量比人体细胞多。文章明确提到“the number of microbial ones is higher – about 39 trillion”，而人体细胞的数量是“about 30 trillion”。这与选项C的意思完全一致。

central_sentences.sentence: What is amazing is that while the number of human cells in the average person is about 30 trillion, the number of microbial ones is higher – about 39 trillion.
central_sentences.translation: 令人惊讶的是，虽然普通人体内的人类细胞数量约为30万亿，但微生物细胞的数量更高，约为39万亿。

target_sen.target_sen: What is amazing is that while the number of human cells in the average person is about 30 trillion, the number of microbial ones is higher – about 39 trillion.

questions.title: What is the writer doing in the fifth paragraph?

options.text: explaining how a discovery was made
giving details of how microbes cause disease

questions.analysis: 文章第五段详细描述了Antony van Leeuwenhoek如何使用显微镜发现微生物的过程，这正是解释一个发现是如何被做出的。因此，正确答案是A。

central_sentences.sentence: The first man to see these extraordinarily potent creatures was a Dutch lens-maker called Antony van Leeuwenhoek in the 1670s. Using microscopes of his own design that could magnify up to 270 times, he examined a drop of water from a nearby lake and found it teeming with tiny creatures he called 'animalcules'.
central_sentences.translation: 1670年代，一位名叫安东尼·范·列文虎克的荷兰镜片制造商成为第一个看到这些非常强大的生物的人。他使用自己设计的可以放大270倍的显微镜，检查了附近一个湖中的一滴水，发现里面充满了他称之为"小动物"的微小生物。

groups.title: We should be more tolerant of microbes
groups.desc:
Questions 17–20

Complete the summary using the list of words, A–H, below.
Choose the correct letter, A–Hand move it into boxes 17–20.
groups.collect:
Yong's book argues that we should be more tolerant of microbes. Many have a beneficial effect, and only a relatively small number lead to $377$. And although it is misleading to think of microbes as 'friendly', we should also stop thinking of them as the enemy. In fact, we should accept that our relationship with microbes is one based on $378$.

New research shows that microbes have numerous benefits for humans. Amongst other things, they aid digestion, remove poisons, produce vitamins and may even help reduce obesity. However, there is a growing problem. Our poor $379$, our overuse of antibiotics, and our excessive focus on $380$are upsetting the bacterial balance and may be contributing to the huge increase in allergies and immune system problems.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“beneficial effect”对应原文中的“play a vital role in maintaining our health”，“relatively small number”对应“fewer than one hundred species”，“lead to”对应“bring”。原文中明确提到“fewer than one hundred species of bacteria bring disease”，因此“disease”是最直接的同义词，而选项中“illness”是“disease”的同义词，所以正确答案是“G: illness”。

central_sentences.sentence: Yong's book is in many ways a plea for microbial tolerance, pointing out that while fewer than one hundred species of bacteria bring disease, many thousands more play a vital role in maintaining our health.
central_sentences.translation: 杨的书在很多方面都是为微生物容忍度辩护，指出虽然少于一百种细菌会导致疾病，但还有成千上万种细菌在维持我们的健康方面起着至关重要的作用。

target_sen.target_sen: Yong's book is in many ways a plea for microbial tolerance, pointing out that while fewer than one hundred species of bacteria bring disease, many thousands more play a vital role in maintaining our health.

Yong's book is in many ways a plea for microbial tolerance, pointing out that while fewer than one hundred species of bacteria bring disease, many thousands more play a vital role in maintaining our health.

Yong's book is in many ways a plea for microbial tolerance, pointing out that while fewer than one hundred species of bacteria bring disease, many thousands more play a vital role in maintaining our health.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“relationship with microbes”在原文中对应的是“we have a symbiotic relationship”。“symbiotic relationship”意为共生关系，即一种合作或伙伴关系。因此，最直接对应的答案是“partnership”。

central_sentences.sentence: Instead we should realise we have a symbiotic relationship, that can be mutually beneficial or mutually destructive.
central_sentences.translation: 相反，我们应该意识到我们与微生物有一种共生关系，这种关系可能是互利的，也可能是互相破坏的。

target_sen.target_sen: Instead we should realise we have a symbiotic relationship, that can be mutually beneficial or mutually destructive.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "poor nutrition" 对应原文中的 "unhealthy, low-fibre diets"。原文明确指出不健康的饮食是导致细菌平衡被破坏的原因之一，这与题干中的 "poor nutrition" 完全一致。因此，正确答案是 "nutrition"。

central_sentences.sentence: Our obsession with hygiene, our overuse of antibiotics and our unhealthy, low-fibre diets are disrupting the bacterial balance and may be responsible for soaring rates of allergies and immune problems, such as inflammatory bowel disease (IBD).
central_sentences.translation: 我们对卫生的痴迷，过度使用抗生素，以及不健康、低纤维的饮食正在破坏细菌平衡，可能导致过敏和免疫问题（如炎症性肠病）发病率的飙升。

target_sen.target_sen: Our obsession with hygiene, our overuse of antibiotics and our unhealthy, low-fibre diets are disrupting the bacterial balance and may be responsible for soaring rates of allergies and immune problems, such as inflammatory bowel disease (IBD).

Our obsession with hygiene, our overuse of antibiotics and our unhealthy, low-fibre diets are disrupting the bacterial balance and may be responsible for soaring rates of allergies and immune problems, such as inflammatory bowel disease (IBD).

Our obsession with hygiene, our overuse of antibiotics and our unhealthy, low-fibre diets are disrupting the bacterial balance and may be responsible for soaring rates of allergies and immune problems, such as inflammatory bowel disease (IBD).

Our obsession with hygiene, our overuse of antibiotics and our unhealthy, low-fibre diets are disrupting the bacterial balance and may be responsible for soaring rates of allergies and immune problems, such as inflammatory bowel disease (IBD).

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "excessive focus on" 在原文中对应的是 "obsession with"，而 "hygiene" 对应的是 "cleanliness"。因此，正确答案是 "cleanliness"。文章中明确提到对卫生的过度关注会破坏细菌平衡，导致过敏和免疫系统问题的增加。

central_sentences.sentence: Our obsession with hygiene, our overuse of antibiotics and our unhealthy, low-fibre diets are disrupting the bacterial balance and may be responsible for soaring rates of allergies and immune problems, such as inflammatory bowel disease (IBD).
central_sentences.translation: 我们对卫生的过度关注，过度使用抗生素，以及不健康的低纤维饮食正在破坏细菌平衡，可能导致过敏和免疫问题（如炎症性肠病）发病率的飙升。

target_sen.target_sen: Our obsession with hygiene, our overuse of antibiotics and our unhealthy, low-fibre diets are disrupting the bacterial balance and may be responsible for soaring rates of allergies and immune problems, such as inflammatory bowel disease (IBD).

groups.desc:
Questions 21–26

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 2?

In boxes 21–26 on your answer sheet, write
YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

questions.title: It is possible that using antibacterial products in the home fails to have the desired effect.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 YES。题干中的 "using antibacterial products in the home" 对应原文中的 "use of household detergents and antibacterial products"，而 "fails to have the desired effect" 对应原文中的 "destroys the microbes that normally keep the more dangerous germs at bay"。原文明确指出，过度使用抗菌产品会破坏有益微生物，这意味着抗菌产品未能达到预期效果。因此，题干所述信息在原文中得到了支持。

central_sentences.sentence: The most recent research actually turns accepted norms upside down. For example, there are studies indicating that the excessive use of household detergents and antibacterial products actually destroys the microbes that normally keep the more dangerous germs at bay.
central_sentences.translation: 最新的研究实际上颠覆了人们普遍接受的观念。例如，有研究表明，过度使用家用清洁剂和抗菌产品实际上会破坏那些通常能够抑制更危险病菌的微生物。

target_sen.target_sen: For example, there are studies indicating that the excessive use of household detergents and antibacterial products actually destroys the microbes that normally keep the more dangerous germs at bay.

syn_ary.synonym: destroys the microbes that normally keep the more dangerous germs at bay

target_sen.target_sen: For example, there are studies indicating that the excessive use of household detergents and antibacterial products actually destroys the microbes that normally keep the more dangerous germs at bay.

locating_words.locating_word: fails to have the desired effect

questions.title: It is a good idea to ensure that children come into contact with as few bacteria as possible.
questions.analysis: 题干中的观点是“确保孩子尽量少接触细菌是个好主意”，而原文中提到“养狗可以让孩子早期接触到多种细菌，这可能有助于他们以后对抗过敏”。这表明接触多种细菌对孩子是有好处的，反驳了题干中的观点。因此，答案是NO。

central_sentences.sentence: Other studies show that keeping a dog as a pet gives children early exposure to a diverse range of bacteria, which may help protect them against allergies later.
central_sentences.translation: 其他研究表明，养狗作为宠物可以让孩子早期接触多种细菌，这可能有助于保护他们日后不受过敏的影响。

target_sen.target_sen: Other studies show that keeping a dog as a pet gives children early exposure to a diverse range of bacteria, which may help protect them against allergies later.
For example, there are studies indicating that the excessive use of household detergents and antibacterial products actually destroys the microbes that normally keep the more dangerous germs at bay.
Other studies show that keeping a dog as a pet gives children early exposure to a diverse range of bacteria, which may help protect them against allergies later.
For example, there are studies indicating that the excessive use of household detergents and antibacterial products actually destroys the microbes that normally keep the more dangerous germs at bay.

questions.title: Yong's book contains more case studies than are necessary.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词“Yong's book”、“case studies”和“more than necessary”在原文中都有对应的同义替换词。然而，原文中并没有提到Yong的书中包含的案例研究数量是否超过了必要的数量。原文只是提到了一些具体的案例研究，但没有评价这些案例研究的数量是否过多。因此，正确答案是NOT GIVEN，因为文章中没有提供足够的信息来支持或反驳题干中的说法。

central_sentences.sentence: The readers of Yong's book must be prepared for a decidedly unglamorous world. Among the less appealing case studies is one about a fungus that is wiping out entire populations of frogs and that can be halted by a rare microbial bacterium. Another is about squid that carry luminescent bacteria that protect them against predators.
central_sentences.translation: 读者必须准备好面对Yong书中描述的一个毫不glamorous的世界。在一些不太吸引人的案例研究中，有一个是关于一种真菌正在毁灭整个蛙类种群，而这种真菌可以被一种罕见的微生物细菌阻止。另一个案例是关于携带发光细菌的鱿鱼，这些细菌可以保护它们免受捕食者的伤害。

target_sen.target_sen: The readers of Yong's book must be prepared for a decidedly unglamorous world.
Among the less appealing case studies is one about a fungus that is wiping out entire populations of frogs and that can be halted by a rare microbial bacterium.

questions.title: The case study about bacteria that prevent squid from being attacked may have limited appeal.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "limited appeal" 对应文章中的 "less appealing"，"bacteria that prevent squid from being attacked" 对应文章中的 "squid that carry luminescent bacteria that protect them against predators"。这些同义替换词明确地表明了题干和文章内容的对应关系，因此答案是 YES。

central_sentences.sentence: The readers of Yong's book must be prepared for a decidedly unglamorous world. Among the less appealing case studies is one about a fungus that is wiping out entire populations of frogs and that can be halted by a rare microbial bacterium. Another is about squid that carry luminescent bacteria that protect them against predators.
central_sentences.translation: 读者必须准备好面对一个毫无glamour（魅力）的世界。在一些不太吸引人的案例研究中，有一个是关于一种真菌正在消灭整个青蛙种群，而这种真菌可以被一种罕见的微生物细菌阻止。另一个是关于鱿鱼携带发光细菌来保护自己免受捕食者攻击。

syn_ary.synonym: squid that carry luminescent bacteria that protect them against predators

target_sen.target_sen: Another is about squid that carry luminescent bacteria that protect them against predators.

locating_words.locating_word: bacteria that prevent squid from being attacked

target_sen.target_sen: Among the less appealing case studies is one about a fungus that is wiping out entire populations of frogs and that can be halted by a rare microbial bacterium.

questions.title: Efforts to control dengue fever have been surprisingly successful.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Efforts to control dengue fever" 对应文章中的 "in an attempt to stop mosquitoes spreading dengue fever"。但是，题干中的 "have been surprisingly successful" 在文章中没有找到直接对应的表达。文章只提到了一种尝试，但没有提到这些努力是否成功。因此，正确答案是 NOT GIVEN，因为文章中没有提到控制登革热的努力是否成功，更没有提到这些努力是否取得了令人惊讶的成功。

central_sentences.sentence: Already, in an attempt to stop mosquitoes spreading dengue fever – a disease that infects 400 million people a year – mosquitoes are being loaded with a bacterium to block the disease.
central_sentences.translation: 已经有尝试通过在蚊子体内注入细菌来阻止登革热的传播——这是一种每年感染4亿人的疾病。

target_sen.target_sen: Already, in an attempt to stop mosquitoes spreading dengue fever – a disease that infects 400 million people a year – mosquitoes are being loaded with a bacterium to block the disease.
Already, in an attempt to stop mosquitoes spreading dengue fever – a disease that infects 400 million people a year – mosquitoes are being loaded with a bacterium to block the disease.

questions.title: Microbes that reduce the risk of infection have already been put inside the walls of some hospital wards.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“reduce the risk of infection”在原文中对应“fight off infections”，“have already been put”在原文中对应“could construct”，而“hospital wards”在原文中对应“buildings”。原文明确表示这是未来的可能性（“In the future”），而不是已经实现的事实。因此，题干中的陈述是错误的，正确答案是NO。

central_sentences.sentence: In the future, our ability to manipulate microbes means we could construct buildings with useful microbes built into their walls to fight off infections.
central_sentences.translation: 在未来，我们操纵微生物的能力意味着我们可以建造建筑物，在其墙壁中植入有用的微生物来抵抗感染。

target_sen.target_sen: In the future, our ability to manipulate microbes means we could construct buildings with useful microbes built into their walls to fight off infections.
Just imagine a neonatal hospital ward coated in a specially mixed cocktail of microbes so that babies get the best start in life.
In the future, our ability to manipulate microbes means we could construct buildings with useful microbes built into their walls to fight off infections.
In the future, our ability to manipulate microbes means we could construct buildings with useful microbes built into their walls to fight off infections.

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Across cultures, wisdom has been considered one of the most revered human qualities. Although the truly wise may seem few and far between, empirical research examining wisdom suggests that it isn't an exceptional trait possessed by a small handful of bearded philosophers after all – in fact, the latest studies suggest that most of us have the ability to make wise decisions, given the right context.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: 'It appears that experiential, situational, and cultural factors are even more powerful in shaping wisdom than previously imagined,' says Associate Professor Igor Grossmann of the University of Waterloo in Ontario, Canada. 'Recent empirical findings from cognitive, developmental, social, and personality psychology cumulatively suggest that people's ability to reason wisely varies dramatically across experiential and situational contexts. Understanding the role of such contextual factors offers unique insights into understanding wisdom in daily life, as well as how it can be enhanced and taught.'

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: It seems that it's not so much that some people simply possess wisdom and others lack it, but that our ability to reason wisely depends on a variety of external factors. 'It is impossible to characterize thought processes attributed to wisdom without considering the role of contextual factors,' explains Grossmann. 'In other words, wisdom is not solely an “inner quality” but rather unfolds as a function of situations people happen to be in. Some situations are more likely to promote wisdom than others.'

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Coming up with a definition of wisdom is challenging, but Grossmann and his colleagues have identified four key characteristics as part of a framework of wise reasoning. One is intellectual humility or recognition of the limits of our own knowledge, and another is appreciation of perspectives wider than the issue at hand. Sensitivity to the possibility of change in social relations is also key, along with compromise or integration of different attitudes and beliefs.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Grossmann and his colleagues have also found that one of the most reliable ways to support wisdom in our own day-to-day decisions is to look at scenarios from a third-party perspective, as though giving advice to a friend. Research suggests that when adopting a first-person viewpoint we focus on 'the focal features of the environment' and when we adopt a third-person, 'observer' viewpoint we reason more broadly and focus more on interpersonal and moral ideals such as justice and impartiality. Looking at problems from this more expansive viewpoint appears to foster cognitive processes related to wise decisions.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: What are we to do, then, when confronted with situations like a disagreement with a spouse or negotiating a contract at work, that require us to take a personal stake? Grossmann argues that even when we aren't able to change the situation, we can still evaluate these experiences from different perspectives.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: For example, in one experiment that took place during the peak of a recent economic recession, graduating college seniors were asked to reflect on their job prospects. The students were instructed to imagine their career either 'as if you were a distant observer' or 'before your own eyes as if you were right there'. Participants in the group assigned to the 'distant observer' role displayed more wisdom-related reasoning (intellectual humility and recognition of change) than did participants in the control group.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: In another study, couples in long-term romantic relationships were instructed to visualize an unresolved relationship conflict either through the eyes of an outsider or from their own perspective. Participants then discussed an incident with their partner for 10 minutes, after which they wrote down their thoughts about it. Couples in the 'other's eyes' condition were significantly more likely to rely on wise reasoning – recognizing others' perspectives and searching for a compromise – compared to the couples in the egocentric condition.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: 'Ego-decentering promotes greater focus on others and enables a bigger picture, conceptual view of the experience, affording recognition of intellectual humility and change,' says Grossmann.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: We might associate wisdom with intelligence or particular personality traits, but research shows only a small positive relationship between wise thinking and crystallized intelligence and the personality traits of openness and agreeableness. 'It is remarkable how much people can vary in their wisdom from one situation to the next, and how much stronger such contextual effects are for understanding the relationship between wise judgment and its social and affective outcomes as compared to the generalized “traits”,' Grossmann explains. 'That is, knowing how wisely a person behaves in a given situation is more informative for understanding their emotions or likelihood to forgive [or] retaliate as compared to knowing whether the person may be wise “in general”.'

groups.desc:
Questions 27–30

Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

questions.title: What point does the writer make in the first paragraph?

options.text: Wisdom appears to be unique to the human race.
A basic assumption about wisdom may be wrong.
Concepts of wisdom may depend on the society we belong to.
There is still much to be discovered about the nature of wisdom.

questions.analysis: 文章中提到的“empirical research examining wisdom suggests that it isn't an exceptional trait possessed by a small handful of bearded philosophers after all”表明了一个基本假设可能是错误的，即智慧并不是少数人独有的特殊品质。这与选项 B 中的“a basic assumption about wisdom may be wrong”直接对应，因此选项 B 是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Although the truly wise may seem few and far between, empirical research examining wisdom suggests that it isn't an exceptional trait possessed by a small handful of bearded philosophers after all – in fact, the latest studies suggest that most of us have the ability to make wise decisions, given the right context.
central_sentences.translation: 尽管真正有智慧的人看似稀少，但是对智慧的实证研究表明，智慧实际上并不是只有少数留着胡子的哲学家才拥有的特殊品质——事实上，最新研究表明，在适当的环境下，我们大多数人都有能力做出明智的决定。

target_sen.target_sen: Although the truly wise may seem few and far between, empirical research examining wisdom suggests that it isn't an exceptional trait possessed by a small handful of bearded philosophers after all – in fact, the latest studies suggest that most of us have the ability to make wise decisions, given the right context.

questions.title: What does Igor Grossmann suggest about the ability to make wise decisions?

options.text: It can vary greatly from one person to another.
Earlier research into it was based on unreliable data.
The importance of certain influences on it was underestimated.
Various branches of psychology define it according to their own criteria.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C: The importance of certain influences on it was underestimated。文章中提到，经验、情境和文化因素在塑造智慧方面比以前想象的更强大，这表明这些因素的重要性被低估了。因此，C选项是正确的。

central_sentences.sentence: 'It appears that experiential, situational, and cultural factors are even more powerful in shaping wisdom than previously imagined,' says Associate Professor Igor Grossmann of the University of Waterloo in Ontario, Canada.
central_sentences.translation: 加拿大安大略省滑铁卢大学的副教授Igor Grossmann说："看来，经验、情境和文化因素在塑造智慧方面比以前想象的更强大。"

target_sen.target_sen: 'Recent empirical findings from cognitive, developmental, social, and personality psychology cumulatively suggest that people's ability to reason wisely varies dramatically across experiential and situational contexts.

'Recent empirical findings from cognitive, developmental, social, and personality psychology cumulatively suggest that people's ability to reason wisely varies dramatically across experiential and situational contexts.

questions.title: According to the third paragraph, Grossmann claims that the level of wisdom an individual shows

options.text: can be greater than they think it is.
will be different in different circumstances.
may be determined by particular aspects of their personality.
should develop over time as a result of their life experiences.

questions.analysis: 根据文章内容，Grossmann认为智慧水平取决于外部因素和情境。文章明确指出智慧不是单纯的内在品质，而是随着情境的变化而变化。因此，选项B“will be different in different circumstances”是正确的，因为它直接反映了文章的主旨，即智慧水平在不同情境下会有所不同。

central_sentences.sentence: It is impossible to characterize thought processes attributed to wisdom without considering the role of contextual factors
central_sentences.translation: 如果不考虑情境因素的作用，就不可能描述归因于智慧的思维过程。

central_sentences.sentence: Some situations are more likely to promote wisdom than others.
central_sentences.translation: 某些情况比其他情况更容易促进智慧。

target_sen.target_sen: It seems that it's not so much that some people simply possess wisdom and others lack it, but that our ability to reason wisely depends on a variety of external factors.
It seems that it's not so much that some people simply possess wisdom and others lack it, but that our ability to reason wisely depends on a variety of external factors.

questions.title: What is described in the fifth paragraph?

options.text: difficulty encountered when attempting to reason wisely
an example of the type of person who is likely to reason wisely
a controversial view about the benefits of reasoning wisely
a recommended strategy that can help people to reason wisely

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 D，因为文章中明确提到了一种推荐的方法，即从第三方视角看待情境，这可以帮助人们做出明智的决策。题干中的 "recommended strategy" 和 "reason wisely" 分别对应文章中的 "reliable ways" 和 "support wisdom"。

central_sentences.sentence: Grossmann and his colleagues have also found that one of the most reliable ways to support wisdom in our own day-to-day decisions is to look at scenarios from a third-party perspective, as though giving advice to a friend.
central_sentences.translation: Grossmann和他的同事们还发现，在我们日常决策中支持智慧的最可靠方法之一是从第三方的角度看待情况，就像给朋友提建议一样。

groups.title: The characteristics of wise reasoning
groups.desc:
Questions 31–35

Complete the summary using the list of words, A–J, below.
Move the correct answer, A–J intoboxes 31–35.
groups.collect:
Igor Grossmann and colleagues have established four characteristics which enable us to make wise decisions. It is important to have a certain degree of $391$regarding the extent of our knowledge, and to take into account $392$which may not be the same as our own. We should also be able to take a broad$393$of any situation. Another key characteristic is being aware of the likelihood of alterations in the way that people relate to each other.

Grossmann also believes that it is better to regard scenarios with $394$. By avoiding the first-person perspective, we focus more on $395$and on other moral ideals, which in turn leads to wiser decision-making.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "extent of our knowledge" 对应文章中的 "limits of our own knowledge"，而 "take into account" 对应 "appreciation of"。因此，题干中的【 】应该填入与 "intellectual humility" 同义的词，即 "modesty"。

central_sentences.sentence: One is intellectual humility or recognition of the limits of our own knowledge, and another is appreciation of perspectives wider than the issue at hand.
central_sentences.translation: 一个是智力上的谦逊或认识到我们自身知识的局限性，另一个是欣赏比眼前问题更广泛的视角。

target_sen.target_sen: One is intellectual humility or recognition of the limits of our own knowledge, and another is appreciation of perspectives wider than the issue at hand.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“extent of our knowledge”对应原文中的“limits of our own knowledge”，“take into account”对应“appreciation of”，“which may not be the same as our own”对应“wider than the issue at hand”。因此，最直接对应的答案是“opinions”，因为“perspectives”和“opinions”在这里是同义表达，意为“观点”。

central_sentences.sentence: One is intellectual humility or recognition of the limits of our own knowledge, and another is appreciation of perspectives wider than the issue at hand.
central_sentences.translation: 一个是智力上的谦逊或认识到我们自身知识的局限性，另一个是欣赏比当前问题更广泛的视角。

target_sen.target_sen: One is intellectual humility or recognition of the limits of our own knowledge, and another is appreciation of perspectives wider than the issue at hand.
One is intellectual humility or recognition of the limits of our own knowledge, and another is appreciation of perspectives wider than the issue at hand.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "broad" 和 "view" 在原文中分别对应 "more broadly" 和 "expansive viewpoint"。这些词语都指向了一个广阔的视角或观点。因此，正确答案是 "view"。

central_sentences.sentence: Looking at problems from this more expansive viewpoint appears to foster cognitive processes related to wise decisions.
central_sentences.translation: 从这种更广阔的视角看问题似乎能促进与明智决策相关的认知过程。

target_sen.target_sen: Research suggests that when adopting a first-person viewpoint we focus on 'the focal features of the environment' and when we adopt a third-person, 'observer' viewpoint we reason more broadly and focus more on interpersonal and moral ideals such as justice and impartiality.
Looking at problems from this more expansive viewpoint appears to foster cognitive processes related to wise decisions.

Research suggests that when adopting a first-person viewpoint we focus on 'the focal features of the environment' and when we adopt a third-person, 'observer' viewpoint we reason more broadly and focus more on interpersonal and moral ideals such as justice and impartiality.
Looking at problems from this more expansive viewpoint appears to foster cognitive processes related to wise decisions.

questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是“objectivity”。题干中的“regard scenarios”在原文中对应的是“look at problems”，而“objectivity”在原文中通过“observer viewpoint”以及“justice and impartiality”来体现。这些词汇都强调了一种客观、公正的态度，因此“objectivity”是最合适的答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Research suggests that when adopting a first-person viewpoint we focus on 'the focal features of the environment' and when we adopt a third-person, 'observer' viewpoint we reason more broadly and focus more on interpersonal and moral ideals such as justice and impartiality.
central_sentences.translation: 研究表明，当我们采用第一人称视角时，我们会关注"环境的焦点特征"，而当我们采用第三人称的"观察者"视角时，我们会更广泛地思考，并更多地关注人际关系和道德理想，如公正和公平。

target_sen.target_sen: Research suggests that when adopting a first-person viewpoint we focus on 'the focal features of the environment' and when we adopt a third-person, 'observer' viewpoint we reason more broadly and focus more on interpersonal and moral ideals such as justice and impartiality.

questions.analysis: 题干中提到“moral ideals”，而文章中提到“moral ideals such as justice and impartiality”。其中，“justice and impartiality”可以理解为“公平和公正”，这与选项中的“fairness”最为接近。因此，正确答案是“fairness”。

central_sentences.sentence: Research suggests that when adopting a first-person viewpoint we focus on 'the focal features of the environment' and when we adopt a third-person, 'observer' viewpoint we reason more broadly and focus more on interpersonal and moral ideals such as justice and impartiality.
central_sentences.translation: 研究表明，当我们采用第一人称视角时，我们关注"环境的焦点特征"；而当我们采用第三人称"观察者"视角时，我们的思考更加广泛，更多地关注人际关系和道德理想，如正义和公正。

target_sen.target_sen: Research suggests that when adopting a first-person viewpoint we focus on 'the focal features of the environment' and when we adopt a third-person, 'observer' viewpoint we reason more broadly and focus more on interpersonal and moral ideals such as justice and impartiality.

Research suggests that when adopting a first-person viewpoint we focus on 'the focal features of the environment' and when we adopt a third-person, 'observer' viewpoint we reason more broadly and focus more on interpersonal and moral ideals such as justice and impartiality.

groups.desc:
Questions 36–40

Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 3?

In questions 36–40 on your answer sheet, choose
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information 
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information 
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this

questions.title: Students participating in the job prospects experiment could choose one of two perspectives to take.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“choose”表示学生有选择权，而原文中的“instructed”表示学生被指示去做某事，没有选择权。因此，题干中的信息与原文不符，正确答案是“FALSE”。

central_sentences.sentence: The students were instructed to imagine their career either 'as if you were a distant observer' or 'before your own eyes as if you were right there'.
central_sentences.translation: 学生们被指示以"远距离观察者"的角度或"如同亲身经历"的角度来想象他们的职业生涯。

target_sen.target_sen: The students were instructed to imagine their career either 'as if you were a distant observer' or 'before your own eyes as if you were right there'.
The students were instructed to imagine their career either 'as if you were a distant observer' or 'before your own eyes as if you were right there'.
Participants in the group assigned to the 'distant observer' role displayed more wisdom-related reasoning (intellectual humility and recognition of change) than did participants in the control group.

questions.title: Participants in the couples experiment were aware that they were taking part in a study about wise reasoning.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息“Participants in the couples experiment were aware that they were taking part in a study about wise reasoning”在原文中没有明确提到。原文只描述了实验的操作过程，但没有提到参与者是否知道他们是在参与一个关于“wise reasoning”的研究。因此，正确答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: In another study, couples in long-term romantic relationships were instructed to visualize an unresolved relationship conflict either through the eyes of an outsider or from their own perspective. Participants then discussed an incident with their partner for 10 minutes, after which they wrote down their thoughts about it. Couples in the 'other's eyes' condition were significantly more likely to rely on wise reasoning – recognizing others' perspectives and searching for a compromise – compared to the couples in the egocentric condition
central_sentences.translation: 在另一项研究中，长期恋爱关系中的伴侣被要求从局外人的角度或自己的角度来想象一个未解决的关系冲突。参与者随后与伴侣讨论了10分钟的事件，之后写下了他们的想法。与自我中心条件下的伴侣相比，"他人视角"条件下的伴侣更有可能依赖于明智的推理——认识到他人的观点并寻求妥协。

target_sen.target_sen: In another study, couples in long-term romantic relationships were instructed to visualize an unresolved relationship conflict either through the eyes of an outsider or from their own perspective.
In another study, couples in long-term romantic relationships were instructed to visualize an unresolved relationship conflict either through the eyes of an outsider or from their own perspective.

Couples in the 'other's eyes' condition were significantly more likely to rely on wise reasoning – recognizing others' perspectives and searching for a compromise – compared to the couples in the egocentric condition.

questions.title: In the couples experiments, the length of the couples' relationships had an impact on the results.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词“couples experiments”、“length of the couples' relationships”和“impact on the results”在原文中都有对应的同义替换词。然而，原文中并没有明确提到“情侣关系的长度对实验结果的影响”，只是提到实验中涉及了长期恋爱关系的情侣。因此，题干中的信息在文章中找不到明确的对应，正确答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: In another study, couples in long-term romantic relationships were instructed to visualize an unresolved relationship conflict either through the eyes of an outsider or from their own perspective. Participants then discussed an incident with their partner for 10 minutes, after which they wrote down their thoughts about it. Couples in the 'other's eyes' condition were significantly more likely to rely on wise reasoning – recognizing others' perspectives and searching for a compromise – compared to the couples in the egocentric condition
central_sentences.translation: 在另一项研究中，长期恋爱关系中的情侣被指示通过旁观者的视角或从自己的角度来想象一个未解决的关系冲突。参与者随后与伴侣讨论一个事件10分钟，之后写下他们的想法。与以自我为中心的情侣相比，处于"他人视角"条件下的情侣更有可能依赖智慧推理——认识到他人的观点并寻求妥协。

syn_ary.synonym: study, couples in long-term romantic relationships

target_sen.target_sen: In another study, couples in long-term romantic relationships were instructed to visualize an unresolved relationship conflict either through the eyes of an outsider or from their own perspective.
In another study, couples in long-term romantic relationships were instructed to visualize an unresolved relationship conflict either through the eyes of an outsider or from their own perspective.

questions.title: In both experiments, the participants who looked at the situation from a more detached viewpoint tended to make wiser decisions.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "detached viewpoint" 对应原文中的 "distant observer" 和 "through the eyes of an outsider"，而 "wiser decisions" 对应 "wisdom-related reasoning" 和 "wise reasoning"。原文明确提到在两个实验中，从更为客观的视角看问题的参与者表现出更高的智慧推理。因此，题干信息与文章内容一致，答案为 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: Participants in the group assigned to the 'distant observer' role displayed more wisdom-related reasoning (intellectual humility and recognition of change) than did participants in the control group.
central_sentences.translation: 被分配到"远距离观察者"角色的参与者比对照组的参与者表现出更多与智慧相关的推理（包括智力谦逊和对变化的认识）。

central_sentences.sentence: Couples in the 'other's eyes' condition were significantly more likely to rely on wise reasoning – recognizing others' perspectives and searching for a compromise – compared to the couples in the egocentric condition.
central_sentences.translation: 与自我中心条件下的夫妇相比，处于"他人视角"条件下的夫妇更有可能依赖于明智的推理——认识他人的观点并寻求妥协。

target_sen.target_sen: The students were instructed to imagine their career either 'as if you were a distant observer' or 'before your own eyes as if you were right there'.
Participants in the group assigned to the 'distant observer' role displayed more wisdom-related reasoning (intellectual humility and recognition of change) than did participants in the control group.

syn_ary.synonym: through the eyes of an outsider

target_sen.target_sen: In another study, couples in long-term romantic relationships were instructed to visualize an unresolved relationship conflict either through the eyes of an outsider or from their own perspective.
Participants in the group assigned to the 'distant observer' role displayed more wisdom-related reasoning (intellectual humility and recognition of change) than did participants in the control group.

Couples in the 'other's eyes' condition were significantly more likely to rely on wise reasoning – recognizing others' perspectives and searching for a compromise – compared to the couples in the egocentric condition.

questions.title: Grossmann believes that a person's wisdom is determined by their intelligence to only a very limited extent.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Grossmann believes that a person's wisdom is determined by their intelligence to only a very limited extent”与原文中的“research shows only a small positive relationship between wise thinking and crystallized intelligence”相对应。原文明确指出智慧与智力之间只有微小的正相关关系，表明智力对智慧的决定作用非常有限。因此，答案是TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: We might associate wisdom with intelligence or particular personality traits, but research shows only a small positive relationship between wise thinking and crystallized intelligence and the personality traits of openness and agreeableness.
central_sentences.translation: 我们可能将智慧与智力或特定的人格特征联系在一起，但研究表明，明智思考与结晶智力以及开放性和亲和力这些人格特征之间只存在微弱的正相关关系。

target_sen.target_sen: We might associate wisdom with intelligence or particular personality traits, but research shows only a small positive relationship between wise thinking and crystallized intelligence and the personality traits of openness and agreeableness.

'It is remarkable how much people can vary in their wisdom from one situation to the next, and how much stronger such contextual effects are for understanding the relationship between wise judgment and its social and affective outcomes as compared to the generalized “traits”,' Grossmann explains.

We might associate wisdom with intelligence or particular personality traits, but research shows only a small positive relationship between wise thinking and crystallized intelligence and the personality traits of openness and agreeableness.

We might associate wisdom with intelligence or particular personality traits, but research shows only a small positive relationship between wise thinking and crystallized intelligence and the personality traits of openness and agreeableness.
